meta.question: Where did the COVID-19 came from?

factors.label: Political/geopolitical obstruction of investigation
factors.rationale: Implies political dynamics hinder definitive origin determination.

provenance.quote: the lab leak theory, and the blame game have been in the headlines again
researchers have a responsibility to carry out a thorough analysis of all possible SARS-CoV-2 origins
Beijing, however, continues to hinder the global investigation, resist sharing information, and blame other countries

Variations in IC analytic views on the origins of the COVID-19 pandemic largely stem from differences in how agencies weigh intelligence reporting and scientific publications and intelligence and scientific gaps.
A call for an independent inquiry into the origin of the SARS-CoV-2 virus
Yet more investigation is still needed to determine the origin of the pandemic.
the theory that SARS-CoV-2 was engineered in and escaped from a lab dominates media attention, even in the absence of strong evidence
we do not have access to some relevant data

Chinese government officials and relevant figures in the scientific community 'possessed some level of awareness of an outbreak of infectious disease' well before that information was disclosed publicly on December 31, 2019
ORIGINS OF COVID‑19: AN EXAMINATION OF AVAILABLE EVIDENCE... COMMITTEE ON HOMELAND SECURITY AND GOVERNMENTAL AFFAIRS
there have not been significant advances on that topic
The report faults the governments of the United States and Germany for not sharing more information from their intelligence communities but reserves its strongest criticism for China.

factors.label: Zoonotic (animal-to-human) transmission
factors.rationale: Source explicitly frames COVID-19 as a zoonotic disease originating from an animal reservoir.

provenance.quote: COVID-19 is a potential zoonotic disease
initial reports from the current novel coronavirus (COVID-19) epidemic in Wuhan, China
SARS-CoV-2 may have originated in an animal reservoir, likely that of bat
SARS-CoV-2 is not a laboratory construct or a purposefully manipulated virus
the emergence of SARS-CoV-2 occurred through the live wildlife trade in China
SARS-CoV-2 emergence likely resulted from multiple zoonotic events
to identify the zoonotic source of the virus and the route of introduction to the human population, including the possible role of intermediate hosts

Four IC elements and the National Intelligence Council assess with low confidence that the initial SARS-CoV-2 infection was most likely caused by natural exposure to an animal infected with it or a close progenitor virus
The National Intelligence Council and four other IC agencies assess that the initial human infection with SARS-CoV-2 most likely was caused by natural exposure to an infected animal
SARS-CoV-2 emerged via the wildlife trade
Theories of accidental release from a lab and zoonotic spillover both remain viable.
The preponderance of scientific evidence indicates a natural origin for SARS-CoV-2.
a natural origin through zoonosis followed by sustained human-to-human spread
The dominant point of view that the virus transferred to humans either directly from bats or through an intermediate mammalian host
the transmission of a natural virus from an infected wildlife mammal... the overall odds ratio is 14,900:1 [against this hypothesis]
SARS-CoV-2 is indeed a naturally occurring virus... the spillover model considered today as the main explanation to zoonotic emergence does not match the virus dynamics
One of them is the zoonotic origin of SARS-CoV-2
most available and accessible published scientific evidence supports that SARS-CoV-2 came to humans from bats or an undetermined intermediate species

factors.label: Geographic/transport hub enabling spread
factors.rationale: Identifies Wuhan's connectivity as a key amplifying factor for the pandemic's spread from its origin.

provenance.quote: Wuhan, China is a large hub connecting the North, South, East and West of China via railways and a major international airport.
the odds that the outbreak would occur in Wuhan, conditional on its location in PRC... estimated as 20:1
point to the Huanan market as the early epicentre of the COVID-19 pandemic

factors.label: Bat coronavirus reservoir
factors.rationale: Source specifically identifies bat as the probable animal reservoir for SARS-CoV-2.

provenance.quote: SARS-CoV-2 may have originated in an animal reservoir, likely that of bat
SARS-CoV-2 seems to have an RBD that binds with high affinity to ACE2 from humans, ferrets, cats and other species with high receptor homology
Recently discovered bat CoVs share higher genetic similarity with SARS‐CoV‐2, than those previously described
several hypotheses have been proposed, there is still no clear explanation about how its causative agent, SARS-CoV-2, emerged in human populations

RaTG13 is the closest related coronavirus genome phylogenetically to SARS-CoV-2... the dataset was generated by the Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV) from a transcriptome derived from Rhinolophus sp. tissue or cell line
future wildlife coronavirus sampling may alter the interpretation of existing data

factors.label: Furin cleavage site as virulence/spillover enabler
factors.rationale: Furin cleavage site is a notable genomic feature discussed in the context of how the virus acquired cross-species capability.

provenance.quote: the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2 has a functional polybasic (furin) cleavage site at the S1–S2 boundary through the insertion of 12 nucleotides
The furin cleavage site in the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2 confers to the virus the ability to cross species and tissue barriers, but was previously unseen in other SARS-like CoVs.
neither the existence of a furin cleavage site in SARS‐CoV‐2, nor the presence of specific sequences within the nucleotide insertion encoding that site are evidence for intelligent design
the FCS has been observed to be under strong selective pressure in humans and confers the enhanced ability to infect some cell types and induce cell–cell fusion

factors.label: Live wildlife/wet market trade as spillover venue
factors.rationale: Identifies the specific mechanism and venue—live mammal vendors at Huanan Market—as the proximate spillover location.

provenance.quote: live SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals were sold at the market in late 2019 and... SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples were spatially associated with vendors selling live mammals
The first zoonotic transmission likely involved lineage B viruses around 18 November 2019... lineage A likely occurred within weeks of this event
including the possible role of intermediate hosts
SARS-CoV-2 emerged via the wildlife trade
the odds of observing the spatiotemporal pattern of confirmed COVID-19 cases with no known link to the specific wholesale market where wildlife mammals were being sold
the most compelling evidence of a possible spillover from animals to humans comes from the independent metagenomic data analysis

the mode falls at the entrance of a parking lot at the market itself, and the 95% confidence region around the mode includes the market. Thus, the market cannot be rejected as central even by SC2024's overly stringent statistical test.

factors.label: Chimeric genome structure (bat + pangolin CoV recombination)
factors.rationale: Chimeric structure could arise from natural recombination or laboratory intervention; source argues this does not rule out lab origin.

provenance.quote: SARS-CoV-2 likely to be chimeric, most of its sequence closest to bat CoV RaTG13, whereas its receptor binding domain (RBD) is almost identical to that of a pangolin CoV

sources.title: Will we ever know where covid-19 came from?

position.quote: Despite another war of words, we aren't any closer to a definitive answer as to where the novel coronavirus came from.

restsOn.quote: asks why

position.quote: these lineages were the result of at least two separate cross-species transmission events into humans... As with other coronaviruses, SARS-CoV-2 emergence likely resulted from multiple zoonotic events.

position.quote: The genetic structure of SARS‐CoV‐2 does not rule out a laboratory origin... Both cleavage site and specific RBD could result from site-directed mutagenesis, a procedure that does not leave a trace.

restsOn.quote: Genomic analyses show SARS-CoV-2 likely to be chimeric, most of its sequence closest to bat CoV RaTG13, whereas its receptor binding domain (RBD) is almost identical to that of a pangolin CoV.

sources.title: Updated Assessment on COVID-19 Origins (ODNI Declassified)

position.quote: the IC remains divided on the most likely origin of COVID-19. All agencies assess that two hypotheses are plausible: natural exposure to an infected animal and a laboratory-associated incident.

restsOn.quote: This assessment is based on information through August 2021.

sources.title: Report on Potential Links Between the Wuhan Institute of Virology and the Origins of COVID-19 (ODNI 2023)

position.quote: The Department of Energy and the Federal Bureau of Investigation assess that a laboratory-associated incident was the most likely cause of the first human infection with SARS-CoV-2 ... All agencies continue to assess that both a natural and laboratory-associated origin remain plausible hypotheses

restsOn.quote: This report outlines the IC's understanding of the WIV, its capabilities, and the actions of its personnel leading up to and in the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sources.title: Investigate the origins of COVID-19

position.quote: Theories of accidental release from a lab and zoonotic spillover both remain viable. Knowing how COVID-19 emerged is critical for informing global strategies to mitigate the risk of future outbreaks.

sources.title: The harms of promoting the lab leak hypothesis for SARS-CoV-2 origins without evidence

position.quote: The preponderance of scientific evidence indicates a natural origin for SARS-CoV-2.

sources.title: A Critical Analysis of the Evidence for the SARS-CoV-2 Origin Hypotheses

position.quote: the origin of SARS-CoV-2 has been the topic of substantial debate primarily because we do not have access to some relevant data. At least two major hypotheses have been suggested: a natural origin through zoonosis followed by sustained human-to-human spread or the introduction of a natural virus into humans from a laboratory source.

restsOn.quote: Here, we summarize the scientific evidence that informs this debate

sources.title: There is still no evidence of SARS‐CoV‐2 laboratory origin: Response to Segreto and Deigin

position.quote: existing genetic data, viral diversity and past human history suggest that a natural origin of the virus is the most likely scenario. Genetic evidence suggesting otherwise is yet to be presented.

restsOn.quote: We show that neither the existence of a furin cleavage site in SARS‐CoV‐2, nor the presence of specific sequences within the nucleotide insertion encoding that site are evidence for intelligent design.

position.quote: What we present below is a substantial body of circumstantial evidence that supports the plausibility of such a scenario... the findings do not offer a definitive answer to the origins question.

restsOn.quote: A Republican-led investigation into the origins of Covid-19 has unearthed additional, though circumstantial, evidence

position.quote: the overall odds ratio is 14,900:1, indicating overwhelming evidence in favor of the hypothesis that the pandemic resulted from an accidental lab leak. This conclusion is robust to alternative specifications of the detailed statistical analysis.

restsOn.quote: the odds of observing the spatiotemporal pattern of confirmed COVID-19 cases with no known link to the specific wholesale market where wildlife mammals were being sold... the odds of observing the spatiotemporal pattern of confirmed vendor cases at that market

position.quote: Our retrospective analysis of scientific data supports the hypothesis that SARS-CoV-2 is indeed a naturally occurring virus.

restsOn.quote: Our retrospective analysis of scientific data

sources.title: SARS-CoV-2's closest relative, RaTG13, was generated from a bat transcriptome not a fecal swab: implications for the origin of COVID-19

position.quote: the data show that the RaTG13 dataset was generated by the Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV) from a transcriptome derived from Rhinolophus sp. tissue or cell line, indicating that RaTG13 was in live culture. This raises the question of whether the WIV was culturing additional unreported coronaviruses closely related to SARS-CoV-2 prior to the pandemic.

restsOn.quote: Metagenomic analysis using Metaxa2 shows that only 10.3% of small subunit (SSU) rRNA sequences in the dataset are bacterial... Assembly of mitochondrial SSU rRNA sequences in the dataset produces a contig 98.7% identical to R.affinis mitochondrial SSU rRNA

sources.title: An updated review of the scientific literature on the origin of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: there is not yet a definitive and well demonstrated conclusion on the origin of SARS-CoV-2... remaining basically the same two hypotheses on it. One of them is the zoonotic origin of SARS-CoV-2, while the second one is the possible leak of this coronavirus from a laboratory.

restsOn.quote: The analysis of the available publications until July 2021 did not allow drawing definitive conclusions on the origin of SARS-CoV-2.

sources.title: The Emergence of the Spike Furin Cleavage Site in SARS-CoV-2 - PMC

position.quote: This has stimulated discussion pertaining to the origin of SARS-CoV-2 because the FCS has been observed to be under strong selective pressure in humans and confers the enhanced ability to infect some cell types and induce cell–cell fusion. Furthermore, scientists have demonstrated interest in studying novel cleavage sites by introducing them into SARSr-CoVs.

restsOn.quote: We review what is known about the SARS-CoV-2 FCS in the context of its pathogenesis, origin, and how future wildlife coronavirus sampling may alter the interpretation of existing data.

sources.title: WHO panel favors natural origin of COVID-19 virus but decries missing evidence

position.quote: it concluded, however, there still is not enough hard evidence to say whether the origin was a natural spillover from animals infected with the coronavirus, SARS-CoV-2, or a leak of the pathogen from a laboratory in the city.

restsOn.quote: Proponents of the natural origin hypothesis point to studies that showed how early cases of COVID-19 clustered around the Huanan Seafood Wholesale Market in Wuhan, where environmental sampling by Chinese scientists after the outbreak surfaced found genetic fragments of both SARS-CoV-2 and mammals susceptible to the virus in the same stalls.

sources.title: Confirmation of the centrality of the Huanan market among early COVID-19 cases

position.quote: our results directly contradict SC2024's and -- together with myriad additional lines of evidence overlooked by SC2024, including crucial epidemiological information -- point to the Huanan market as the early epicentre of the COVID-19 pandemic.

restsOn.quote: The centrality of Wuhan's Huanan market in maps of December 2019 COVID-19 case residential locations, established by Worobey et al. (2022a)
